Section Heading 1: A Belgravia townhouse refitted with considered restraint.
Section Heading 2: Refined interiors worthy of one of London's finest addresses.
Section Paragraph 2: Belgravia Townhouse is a six-storey stucco-fronted property in the heart of one of London's most celebrated residential neighbourhoods. Commissioned by an international client with a particular appreciation for European craft traditions, the project required a full interior fit-out that would express a quiet confidence through the quality of its materials and the intelligence of its spatial planning. The brief was deliberately understated — no excess, no unnecessary decoration, only the best version of each element in its right place.
Section Paragraph 3: The joinery specification for Belgravia Townhouse is among the most complex we have undertaken, with bespoke cabinetry designed for every principal room in the house. The kitchen cabinetry was produced by a French atelier and installed over three days by their own specialist team. Bathroom fittings were sourced from independent makers in Italy and Portugal, with each wet room conceived as a distinct material composition rather than a repeated template.
Section Heading 3: Every room resolved to the highest possible standard.
Section Heading 4: A home that will be cherished across generations.
Section Paragraph 4: Belgravia Townhouse was received with enormous satisfaction by our client, who described the finished result as exceeding every expectation. The project was completed to programme despite the complexity of the joinery and specification coordination involved, a reflection of the depth of experience within our project management team. We continue to maintain a relationship with the client for ongoing care and maintenance of the property.

Section Heading 1: Structural expertise at the core of a complex Blackfriars brief.
Section Heading 2: Structural intervention enabling a bold architectural vision.
Section Paragraph 2: Blackfriars Studio is a compact but architecturally ambitious commission in which structural works formed the primary scope of our appointment. Studio Black Ltd, an architectural practice with a strong position in the London residential market, required a series of complex structural interventions to open up a multi-storey building for a combined studio and residential use. The project demanded close coordination between our structural team, the appointed engineer, and the client's own architects throughout every stage.
Section Paragraph 3: The primary structural challenge involved the introduction of a series of new steel transfer beams to allow the removal of internal load-bearing walls across two floors, creating large open-plan volumes suited to the studio use at ground and mezzanine level. New openings were formed in the existing masonry facade to introduce additional glazing, with steel frames carefully sized and detailed to integrate with the existing structure without any visible interruption to the new interior aesthetic.
Section Heading 3: Engineering precision in service of spatial ambition.
Section Heading 4: A structurally transformed building with architectural integrity.
Section Paragraph 4: Blackfriars Studio was completed within the 18-week programme, with all structural works signed off by the appointed engineer and building control without amendment. The client has since occupied the building as their primary London studio and has given extensive feedback on the quality of the structural works and the care taken to protect the integrity of their design vision throughout. A follow-on structural commission on an adjacent property is currently in the planning stage.

Section Heading 1: Category A delivery in one of London's most vibrant quarters.
Section Heading 2: Positioned to attract the best of London's professional occupiers.
Section Paragraph 2: Borough Market Office sits in a prime position within the SE1 commercial district, a neighbourhood that has rapidly established itself as one of the most sought-after business addresses in London. Southwark & Co commissioned us to deliver a full Category A fit-out across six floors that would enable the building to compete with the best of the Southwark market on quality and specification. Our approach was grounded in a careful reading of the building's proportions and orientation, using these to inform every decision about floor finish, lighting, and services distribution.
Section Paragraph 3: The 22-week programme was structured to allow the client to market the building during the final stages of construction, enabling early engagement with potential tenants before practical completion. Our site team maintained exemplary standards of presentation throughout, ensuring the building always showed at its best during client and agent visits. All works were coordinated closely with the base building team to ensure full integration of the Cat A elements with the building's primary structure and services.
Section Heading 3: A Cat A base build that enabled early market engagement.
Section Heading 4: Fully let ahead of practical completion.
Section Paragraph 4: Borough Market Office achieved full occupancy before the formal completion date, with one tenant taking two floors based on a site visit during the final weeks of construction. The speed of letting reflects both the quality of the location and the confidence that Southwark & Co and their agents had in the specification of the fit-out. We have since been approached by a neighbouring landlord in the same street for a similar Cat A commission.

Section Heading 1: A world-class Cat A office ready for global financial occupiers.
Section Heading 2: Raising the bar for Grade A delivery in Canary Wharf.
Section Paragraph 2: Canary Wharf HQ is a major Category A refurbishment commissioned by Apex Capital Group as part of a broader repositioning of their London commercial portfolio. The 78,000 sq ft project spans four floors of a landmark tower and was designed to attract financial and professional services occupiers seeking the very best in base build quality and specification. Our scope covered all primary and secondary fit-out works, from structural coordination through to decoration and commissioning of all building services.
Section Paragraph 3: Managing a 78,000 sq ft Cat A project across a live commercial tower required meticulous planning and an experienced site management team. Works were phased floor by floor to accommodate the building's existing occupiers, with temporary hoarding and dust control measures maintained throughout. A weekly client reporting protocol was established from week one, ensuring Apex Capital Group had full visibility of progress, cost, and quality at all times.
Section Heading 3: Complex delivery managed with precision across four floors.
Section Heading 4: A fully let asset within six weeks of practical completion.
Section Paragraph 4: Canary Wharf HQ achieved practical completion on programme and was fully let within six weeks of handover, with two financial services firms taking floors directly upon opening. The letting agent cited the quality of the base build as central to the speed of leasing activity, and Apex Capital Group have since confirmed plans to appoint us on two further assets within their London portfolio. The project stands as one of our most commercially successful Cat A commissions to date.

Section Heading 1: A Chelsea family home rebuilt around how people really live.
Section Heading 2: Generous, light-filled spaces designed for family life.
Section Paragraph 2: Chelsea Residence is a six-bedroom family home spread across three floors of a substantial Victorian terraced property. Our client, a growing family with three young children, wanted to transform what had become a fragmented and outdated interior into an open, light-filled home that would grow with them over the coming decades. The design was built around an understanding of how the family actually moves through and uses the house across the course of a typical day, with each space considered both in isolation and in relation to those around it.
Section Paragraph 3: The primary design move was the removal of a series of internal walls to the ground floor to create a single open-plan family kitchen, dining, and living space that opens directly onto a south-facing garden. This required a series of new structural steel elements coordinated with the appointed engineer, and was managed carefully to protect the integrity of the historic facade and party walls. A comprehensive joinery package throughout the home — including the kitchen, utility, and all bedroom storage — was designed in-house and fabricated by a trusted specialist partner.
Section Heading 3: Structural ambition in service of an open, generous home.
Section Heading 4: A home the family fell in love with the moment they moved in.
Section Paragraph 4: Chelsea Residence was handed over on schedule following a 15-week build that the client described as the most professionally managed project they had experienced. The family moved in immediately upon completion and have since reported the transformed ground floor as the primary space for both daily family life and entertaining. We were invited back within three months to advise on the landscaping of the rear garden, and continue to support the client as a trusted advisor for the property.

Section Heading 1: A design-led Cat B workplace for a creative London landlord.
Section Heading 2: Where Clerkenwell's creative spirit meets workplace rigour.
Section Paragraph 2: Clerkenwell Works sits within one of London's most architecturally rich neighbourhoods, and our client wanted the fit-out to reflect that context with intelligence and confidence. The 34,000 sq ft Cat B project was developed for a creative technology company moving from a fragmented set of offices across the borough into a single unified headquarters. Our brief was to create a workplace that could accommodate focused work, collaborative exchange, and client-facing functions within a single coherent spatial strategy.
Section Paragraph 3: The design draws on the warehouse typologies of the surrounding area, with a palette of exposed brickwork, raw steel, and warm-toned timber lending the space an authenticity that resonates with the occupier's creative identity. A series of bespoke meeting rooms, each designed with a distinct material treatment, punctuate the open-plan floor plate and give the office a rich sense of variety and discovery. Acoustic performance was treated with particular care, with engineered timber ceiling panels absorbing sound across the primary work zones.
Section Heading 3: A headquarters that reflects the culture of its occupier.
Section Heading 4: Completed on budget and immediately celebrated by the team.
Section Paragraph 4: Clerkenwell Works was delivered on programme across a 24-week build, with a phased handover that allowed the client to relocate teams in stages. Feedback from occupants following move-in has been exceptional, with particular praise for the acoustic quality of the workspace and the variety of settings available throughout the day. The project has since featured in two workplace design publications and won a regional award for commercial interior design.

Section Heading 1: A landmark commercial fit-out in the heart of the city.
Section Heading 2: Precision at every scale and detail.
Section Paragraph 2: Huett & Grange presented a rare opportunity to transform a prominent commercial floor plate in the City of London. The brief called for a high-specification fit-out that could support a dynamic, multi-tenant retail environment while maintaining an elevated architectural standard. Our team worked closely with Charles & CO from concept through to completion, coordinating all trades across a phased programme without disruption to the surrounding building. The result is a refined commercial environment that sets a new benchmark for the client's portfolio.
Section Paragraph 3: From the outset, we prioritised a collaborative approach with the client and their design team, ensuring every decision was rooted in both commercial logic and design integrity. Material selection was guided by durability, sustainability, and aesthetic coherence, while bespoke joinery details throughout the space add a sense of craft to the broader fit-out. Each zone within the floor plate was carefully considered to balance functionality with visual flow, creating a seamless experience across the entire 82,000 sq ft.
Section Heading 4: Transforming a City floor plate into a benchmark space.
Section Paragraph 4: The completed fit-out has been received exceptionally well by both the client and end occupants. The space now operates as one of the most refined commercial destinations in its immediate area, drawing praise for its material quality and spatial planning. Charles & CO have since engaged our studio on two further projects within their portfolio, reflecting the strength of the working relationship established throughout this commission.

Section Heading 1: A discreet Kensington mews house reborn with quiet elegance.
Section Heading 2: Understated luxury across a historic Kensington property.
Section Paragraph 2: Kensington Mews is a beautifully proportioned mid-terrace mews house set within one of the borough's most sought-after conservation areas. Our client, a London-based family returning from a decade abroad, wanted to bring the property up to a contemporary standard of comfort and finish without erasing any of its historic character. The result is an interior that feels entirely of today while remaining deeply respectful of the building's original scale and detail.
Section Paragraph 3: Material selection was guided by a desire for restraint and longevity. Aged oak herringbone flooring was installed throughout the ground floor, complemented by hand-plastered walls finished in a series of soft, chalky tones that shift in quality as natural light moves through the house across the day. The kitchen was developed in close collaboration with a bespoke furniture maker based in the East End, with painted cabinetry, an integrated larder, and a solid stone worktop forming the centrepiece of the ground-floor plan.
Section Heading 3: Materials chosen for longevity, beauty, and quiet confidence.
Section Heading 4: A family home delivered with precision and care.
Section Paragraph 4: The project was completed within the agreed 14-week programme, coordinating carefully with the conservation officer and the client's structural engineer throughout. Works were managed sensitively given the proximity of neighbouring properties, with all noisy operations scheduled within agreed hours. The family moved in on schedule and have since commissioned us to return for a garden studio project on the same site.

Section Heading 1: A Mayfair penthouse finished to the very highest standard.
Section Heading 2: An address defined by exceptional craft and material quality.
Section Paragraph 2: The Mayfair Penthouse is a duplex apartment occupying the top two floors of a newly constructed residential tower in one of London's most prestigious postcodes. Our client, an international collector with homes across Europe, required a fit-out that would meet the standards of some of the world's finest private residences while expressing a personal aesthetic rooted in a love of contemporary art and European craft. The project was developed over a six-month design period before works commenced on site.
Section Paragraph 3: Stone was the primary material language of the project, with large-format slabs of book-matched marble forming the defining surfaces in the principal living and dining rooms. A specialist stone mason was appointed to manage the cutting, sequencing, and installation of all stone throughout, working to tolerances of less than a millimetre across the primary wall panels. Bespoke lighting was designed by an independent lighting studio in collaboration with our team, with all fittings specified for maximum discretion within the architectural surfaces.
Section Heading 3: Stone, light, and craft at the highest residential level.
Section Heading 4: A penthouse that met every expectation of a demanding brief.
Section Paragraph 4: The Mayfair Penthouse was handed over within the agreed 11-week programme following an intensive pre-construction period during which all materials were sourced, approved, and ordered in advance of works commencing. The client's representative visited the site weekly throughout the build and provided continuous positive feedback on the quality of the work being delivered. The project has since been photographed for an international interiors publication at the client's request.
Featured title: This is a Test. Please Look Carefully

Section Heading 1: A complete Cat A transformation ready for any occupier.
Section Heading 2: Setting the standard for Cat A delivery in Farringdon.
Section Paragraph 2: Meridian House is a Grade A commercial building in Farringdon, repositioned by Meridian Estates to attract a new generation of professional occupiers. Our scope covered the full Category A fit-out across five floors, including raised access flooring, suspended ceilings, mechanical and electrical services, and full decoration throughout. The brief demanded a high-quality base build that could be adapted by any incoming tenant with minimal additional work, giving the client maximum lettability from day one.
Section Paragraph 3: Category A delivery at this scale requires an exceptionally disciplined approach to programme management. Our team coordinated all trades simultaneously across multiple floors, using a floor-by-floor sequencing strategy to maintain consistent progress throughout the 16-week build period. All finishes were specified to align with the building's contemporary architectural character, with particular attention paid to core and corridor areas that would be shared between tenants and therefore require a higher level of finish.
Section Heading 3: Programme-led delivery without compromise to specification.
Section Heading 4: A Grade A asset ready to attract premier occupiers.
Section Paragraph 4: Meridian House completed on programme and has since been fully let to two professional services occupiers, with both tenants taking the space with minimal modification to the Cat A base. The quality of the fit-out was cited by the letting agent as a key differentiator in the competitive Farringdon market, supporting a rental level above initial projections. Meridian Estates have appointed us on their next development in the same borough.

Section Heading 1: A Cat B fit-out built for the energy of East London creativity.
Section Heading 2: Workspace as generous and energetic as the neighbourhood itself.
Section Paragraph 2: Shoreditch Creative Hub is a multi-tenant workspace development in the heart of one of London's most dynamic creative districts. East End Ventures tasked us with delivering a Cat B fit-out for a building that would attract small and medium-sized creative businesses looking for a workspace that genuinely reflects and amplifies their culture. Our response was characterised by openness, raw material honesty, and a commitment to providing a wide range of work settings that give each occupier flexibility throughout their working day.
Section Paragraph 3: The fit-out celebrates the existing building fabric rather than concealing it. Exposed concrete soffits, unpainted brickwork, and polished screed floors form the backdrop against which a series of brightly coloured joinery and acoustic elements create zones of identity and energy. A large central collaboration area serves as the social heart of the building, with a bespoke kitchen island, tiered seating, and a curated materials library providing both practical and cultural amenity for all tenants.
Section Heading 3: Celebrating raw materials and the spirit of Shoreditch.
Section Heading 4: A hub that was fully occupied before practical completion.
Section Paragraph 4: Shoreditch Creative Hub was fully let prior to practical completion, with the final tenant signing their lease during the last week of the build programme. East End Ventures attribute the pre-let success in large part to the quality and authenticity of the fit-out, which was used extensively in their marketing materials during the letting campaign. The building now hosts eighteen creative businesses and has been featured in the Evening Standard's round-up of London's best new workspaces.

Section Heading 1: Structural expertise enabling the transformation of a City asset.
Section Heading 2: Unlocking an underutilised building through structural innovation.
Section Paragraph 2: The Atlas Building is a substantial 1970s commercial property in Old Street that Atlas Property Group acquired with ambitions to reposition as a premium creative workspace. The building's original structure — a concrete frame with deep floor plates — presented significant challenges for natural light penetration and spatial flexibility. Our structural scope was therefore critical to enabling the client's vision, involving the introduction of a series of new atrium openings, facade modifications, and transfer structures to support a comprehensive reconfiguration of the building's floor plates.
Section Paragraph 3: The principal structural challenge was the formation of a new six-storey atrium through the centre of the building, a move that required the careful removal of existing concrete slabs and the installation of new edge beams and transfer structures at each affected level. Works were sequenced floor by floor from the top down to manage temporary loading conditions at each stage, with the appointed structural engineer providing weekly sign-off on the completed works before the team advanced to the next level.
Section Heading 3: Complex structural works delivered safely and without incident.
Section Heading 4: A transformed building unlocked for a new generation of occupiers.
Section Paragraph 4: The Atlas Building's structural transformation was completed within the 26-week programme without a single lost-time incident, a significant achievement given the complexity and risk profile of the works involved. The completed atrium has fundamentally changed the character of the building, introducing natural light deep into the previously dark floor plates and creating a dramatic central space that now serves as the primary social and circulation core. Atlas Property Group have described the structural delivery as exceptional and have retained us for the subsequent Cat A fit-out of the refurbished floors.

Section Heading 1: A historic City building reimagined for contemporary working life.
Section Heading 2: Heritage architecture and modern fit-out in perfect balance.
Section Paragraph 2: The Belfry is a striking Victorian warehouse in the City of London, originally constructed in the 1890s and now sensitively repositioned by Belfry Developments as a premium commercial destination. Our commission covered the complete internal fit-out across seven floors, navigating the constraints of a Grade II listed structure while delivering a workspace specification befitting the City's most demanding occupiers. The project stands as perhaps our most technically complex and architecturally rewarding commission to date.
Section Paragraph 3: Working within a listed building demanded close engagement with the local planning authority and Historic England from the earliest stages of design. Every intervention — from the routing of new mechanical services to the specification of internal partitions — was developed with reference to the significance of the existing fabric and the principle of minimal and reversible change. Original cast iron columns, timber beams, and brick reveals were restored and celebrated rather than concealed, becoming defining features of the completed interior.
Section Heading 3: A sensitive intervention that enriches rather than erases.
Section Heading 4: A project that will define a City block for decades to come.
Section Paragraph 4: The Belfry was completed to programme and has since achieved full occupancy, with four occupiers taking space across the seven floors. The project received a commendation at the RICS London Awards and has been featured in three major industry publications. For Belfry Developments, it represents the most significant asset in their portfolio; for our studio, it represents the fullest expression of what we believe contemporary fit-out can achieve when history and innovation are held in equal regard.

Section Heading 1: A Cat B fit-out celebrating the spirit of making and craft.
Section Heading 2: Craft and creativity built into the fabric of the workplace.
Section Paragraph 2: The Foundry is the London headquarters of a multi-disciplinary creative studio whose work spans film, brand, and spatial design. Having outgrown their previous office, Foundry Creative Ltd chose a raw warehouse unit in Bermondsey and asked us to realise a workplace that felt as creatively charged as the work produced within it. Our response drew on the studio's ethos of making and process, embedding craft into every element of the interior — from hand-formed concrete reception desks to a library wall of raw-edge timber shelving.
Section Paragraph 3: Close collaboration with the client's own creative directors was integral to our process on this project. The design evolved through a series of workshops in which materials, references, and prototype details were tested and refined together, ensuring the final fit-out genuinely expressed the studio's identity rather than a generic interpretation of it. Sub-contractors were selected for their specialist craft credentials, including a ceramicist who produced the tiling throughout the kitchen and bathroom cores.
Section Heading 3: A studio designed by creatives, for creatives.
Section Heading 4: A workspace as distinctive as the studio that occupies it.
Section Paragraph 4: The Foundry opened to immediate acclaim within the creative community, with the client sharing the space widely across social channels upon completion. Staff retention has improved significantly since the move, with surveys indicating strong satisfaction with the quality and variety of workspaces available. For us, The Foundry represents a model for how the fit-out process can itself become a creative act when client and studio share a common language.

Section Heading 1: A biophilic workplace designed to inspire and retain talent.
Section Heading 2: Where nature and workspace meet in harmony.
Section Paragraph 2: The Gardens project was built around a single organising idea: that the workplace should feel as restorative as it is productive. Working with Harold & CO, we developed a fit-out strategy that introduced extensive biophilic elements throughout the 66,000 sq ft floor plate, from living green walls in the reception and breakout zones to natural timber ceiling baffles that soften the acoustic environment. Every material palette was chosen to reinforce a connection to the natural world without compromising the commercial rigour the client required.
Section Paragraph 3: Delivered within a 24-week programme, the project demanded careful sequencing across multiple workstreams simultaneously. Our site management team coordinated specialist sub-contractors including bespoke joinery fabricators, M&E engineers, and a dedicated horticulture consultant responsible for the living plant installations. The result was a phased handover that allowed the client to mobilise teams into completed zones ahead of the full programme end, minimising their downtime and maximising return on the fit-out investment.
Section Heading 3: Delivered on time without compromise to quality.
Section Heading 4: A workplace that performs as beautifully as it looks.
Section Paragraph 4: Since completion, The Gardens has become one of Harold & CO's flagship commercial assets, cited regularly in industry press as an example of best-in-class workplace design. Occupancy rates have exceeded projections, and occupant wellbeing scores gathered post-occupation reflect the success of the biophilic strategy at its core. For us, The Gardens represents exactly the kind of project where design ambition and commercial performance align perfectly.

Section Heading 1: A flagship mixed-use development delivered with vision and precision.
Section Heading 2: An ambitious mixed-use development at the heart of Notting Hill.
Section Paragraph 2: The Ivory is Ivory Lane Developments' most significant commission in London to date — a mixed-use building in Notting Hill comprising retail, office, and residential components across 67,000 sq ft. Our appointment covered the full fit-out of all three uses, a scope that required a highly flexible design and delivery approach capable of addressing very different functional and aesthetic demands within a single building. The project was developed over a twelve-month period from initial briefing through to completion.
Section Paragraph 3: Managing the intersection of three distinct uses within a single building is among the most complex challenges a fit-out contractor can face, requiring careful zoning, independent servicing strategies, and a materials palette capable of expressing coherence at the building level whilst accommodating the individual requirements of each use. Our design team developed an overarching architectural language — characterised by pale stone, white render, and warm brass detailing — that runs throughout the building and gives The Ivory its name and its identity.
Section Heading 3: Three uses, one coherent and compelling architectural vision.
Section Heading 4: A development that has already become a local landmark.
Section Paragraph 4: The Ivory was received with significant acclaim upon completion, attracting coverage in both the architectural and lifestyle press. All three components were occupied within two months of handover, with the retail units let to two independent brands whose aesthetic aligns closely with the character of the building. Ivory Lane Developments have confirmed that The Ivory will serve as the design and specification benchmark for all future projects within their portfolio, and we are proud to be part of that continued ambition.

Section Heading 1: An exercise in restraint, luxury, and material precision.
Section Heading 2: Redefining luxury residential fit-out in Kensington.
Section Paragraph 2: The Onyx Residence stands as one of our most ambitious residential commissions to date. Engaged by Crisp & CO to deliver the full fit-out of a landmark development in Kensington, our brief was to create interiors that could sit comfortably among the finest residences in London. The material palette revolves around honed black granite, smoked oak, and brushed bronze — a restrained but deeply considered combination that gives the project its name and its character. Every junction, detail, and surface was resolved to the same exacting standard throughout.
Section Paragraph 3: The Onyx Residence demanded a level of craft typically reserved for bespoke residential commissions. Bespoke joinery was fabricated in collaboration with a specialist London workshop, with hand-finished cabinetry throughout the kitchen and living volumes. Stone was sourced directly from European quarries and cut to precise tolerances on site to ensure perfect alignment across all wall and floor panels. The attention to detail embedded in each space was not incidental — it was the entire point of the project.
Section Heading 3: Crafted to the highest residential standard in London.
Section Heading 4: A project that has defined our residential practice.
Section Paragraph 4: The Onyx Residence has become the reference point for our residential offer, demonstrating what is possible when a client and studio share the same uncompromising vision. The project was completed within the 19-week programme and has since been submitted for two major industry awards. Crisp & CO have described the collaboration as one of the most productive they have experienced, and we continue to work with them across their wider development pipeline.

Section Heading 1: A raw Soho warehouse transformed into a refined urban home.
Section Heading 2: Industrial character meets domestic warmth and craft.
Section Paragraph 2: The Soho Loft began as a raw warehouse shell in the heart of one of London's most culturally rich neighbourhoods. Our client — a creative professional relocating from abroad — wanted a home that would honour the building's industrial heritage while providing the warmth and comfort of a truly considered domestic environment. The design response balances exposed concrete ceilings and steel windows with rich timber floors, bespoke joinery, and hand-selected stone surfaces throughout.
Section Paragraph 3: Bespoke joinery was central to the project, with a kitchen island fabricated in black oxidised steel and honed Carrara marble forming the centrepiece of the open-plan living volume. A custom steel staircase was designed and fabricated by a local metalworker, connecting the main living level to a mezzanine study and bedroom suite above. Throughout, lighting was treated as an architectural element in its own right, with a layered scheme designed to animate the space across morning, day, and evening conditions.
Section Heading 3: Bespoke craft woven into every surface and detail.
Section Heading 4: A one-of-a-kind home in the heart of Soho.
Section Paragraph 4: The Soho Loft was delivered within a 12-week programme that required careful coordination with the building's management given its location within a listed mixed-use block. The client moved in on schedule and has since referred two further residential commissions to our studio. The project now features regularly in our portfolio as an example of how industrial architecture can be sensitively transformed into an exceptional private home.

Section Heading 1: Flexible workspace at scale for a global occupier.
Section Heading 2: Designing flexibility into every square foot.
Section Paragraph 2: The We Work commission represented our largest single-floor fit-out to date, covering 99,000 sq ft across a prominent commercial building in Canary Wharf. Jet Holdings required a fit-out that could serve the full spectrum of co-working occupiers — from solo operators to enterprise-level teams — within a single coherent architectural language. Our design response layered a series of scalable, demountable workspace configurations over a robust base build, allowing the operator maximum flexibility in how they programme and lease the space.
Section Paragraph 3: Given the scale and occupier diversity of the brief, our approach prioritised systems thinking over purely aesthetic concerns. Acoustic zoning, lighting control, and spatial modularity were all designed with the operator's long-term management requirements in mind. A central material palette of raw concrete, micro-steel, and warm timber was applied consistently across all zones, giving the space a recognisable identity while allowing individual areas to have their own distinct character.
Section Heading 3: Systems thinking at the heart of a complex brief.
Section Heading 4: A co-working destination built for long-term performance.
Section Paragraph 4: We Work has been one of Jet Holdings' most successful launches, reaching 80% occupancy within three months of opening. The operational flexibility built into the fit-out has allowed the client to respond quickly to tenant demand across different suite configurations, demonstrating the value of the modular design strategy. Our team is proud to have delivered the project on time and within budget across a complex, live building environment.
